root_text.sn51.22:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn51.22:1.3: “abhijānāti nu kho, bhante, bhagavā iddhiyā manomayena kāyena brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamitā”ti?
root_text.sn51.22:1.4: “Abhijānāmi khvāhaṁ, ānanda, iddhiyā manomayena kāyena brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamitā”ti.
root_text.sn51.22:1.5: “Abhijānāti pana, bhante, bhagavā iminā cātumahābhūtikena kāyena iddhiyā brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamitā”ti?
root_text.sn51.22:1.6: “Abhijānāmi khvāhaṁ, ānanda, iminā cātumahābhūtikena kāyena iddhiyā brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamitā”ti.
root_text.sn51.22:2.1: “Yañca kho omāti, bhante, bhagavā iddhiyā manomayena kāyena brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamituṁ, yañca kho abhijānāti, bhante, bhagavā iminā cātumahābhūtikena kāyena iddhiyā brahmalokaṁ upasaṅkamitā, tayidaṁ, bhante, bhagavato acchariyañceva abbhutañcā”ti.
root_text.sn51.22:2.2: “Acchariyā ceva, ānanda, tathāgatā acchariyadhammasamannāgatā ca, abbhutā ceva, ānanda, tathāgatā abbhutadhammasamannāgatā ca.
root_text.sn51.22:3.1: Yasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgato kāyampi citte samodahati cittampi kāye samodahati, sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca kāye okkamitvā viharati;
root_text.sn51.22:3.2: tasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgatassa kāyo lahutaro ceva hoti mudutaro ca kammaniyataro ca pabhassarataro ca.
root_text.sn51.22:4.1: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, ayoguḷo divasaṁ santatto lahutaro ceva hoti mudutaro ca kammaniyataro ca pabhassarataro ca;
root_text.sn51.22:4.2: evameva kho, ānanda, yasmiṁ samaye tathāgato kāyampi citte samodahati, cittampi kāye samodahati, sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca kāye okkamitvā viharati;
root_text.sn51.22:4.3: tasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgatassa kāyo lahutaro ceva hoti mudutaro ca kammaniyataro ca pabhassarataro ca.
root_text.sn51.22:5.1: Yasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgato kāyampi citte samodahati, cittampi kāye samodahati, sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca kāye okkamitvā viharati;
root_text.sn51.22:5.2: tasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgatassa kāyo appakasireneva pathaviyā vehāsaṁ abbhuggacchati,
root_text.sn51.22:5.3: so anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhoti—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hoti, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hoti …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteti.
root_text.sn51.22:6.1: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, tūlapicu vā kappāsapicu vā lahuko vātūpādāno appakasireneva pathaviyā vehāsaṁ abbhuggacchati;
root_text.sn51.22:6.2: evameva kho, ānanda, yasmiṁ samaye tathāgato kāyampi citte samodahati, cittampi kāye samodahati, sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca kāye okkamitvā viharati;
root_text.sn51.22:6.3: tasmiṁ, ānanda, samaye tathāgatassa kāyo appakasireneva pathaviyā vehāsaṁ abbhuggacchati,
root_text.sn51.22:6.4: so anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhoti—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hoti …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vattetī”ti.

translation_text.sn51.22:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn51.22:1.3: “Sir, do you have personal experience of going to the realm of divinity by psychic power with a mind-made body?”
translation_text.sn51.22:1.5: “But do you have personal experience of going to the realm of divinity by psychic power with this body made up of the four principal states?”
translation_text.sn51.22:2.1: “It’s incredible and amazing that the Buddha is capable of going to the realm of divinity by psychic power with a mind-made body! And that he has personal experience of going to the realm of divinity by psychic power with this body made up of the four principal states!”
translation_text.sn51.22:2.2: “Ānanda, the Realized Ones are incredible and have incredible qualities. They’re amazing and have amazing qualities.
translation_text.sn51.22:3.1: Sometimes the Realized One submerges his body in his mind and his mind in his body. He meditates after sinking into a perception of bliss and lightness in the body.
translation_text.sn51.22:3.2: At that time his body becomes lighter, softer, more workable, and more radiant.
translation_text.sn51.22:4.1: Suppose there was an iron ball that had been heated all day. It’d become lighter, softer, more workable, and more radiant.
translation_text.sn51.22:4.2: In the same way, sometimes the Realized One submerges his body in his mind and his mind in his body. He meditates after sinking into a perception of bliss and lightness in the body.
translation_text.sn51.22:4.3: At that time his body becomes lighter, softer, more workable, and more radiant.
translation_text.sn51.22:5.1: Sometimes the Realized One submerges his body in his mind and his mind in his body. He meditates after sinking into a perception of bliss and lightness in the body.
translation_text.sn51.22:5.2: At that time his body easily rises up from the ground into the air.
translation_text.sn51.22:5.3: He wields the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying himself and becoming one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn51.22:6.1: Suppose there was a light tuft of cotton-wool or kapok. Taken up by the wind, it would easily rise up from the ground into the air.
translation_text.sn51.22:6.2: In the same way, sometimes the Realized One submerges his body in his mind and his mind in his body. He meditates after sinking into a perception of bliss and lightness in the body.
translation_text.sn51.22:6.3: At that time his body easily rises up from the ground into the air.
translation_text.sn51.22:6.4: He wields the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying himself and becoming one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.”
